{"key_event":"Liberal reforms and Labour’s parliamentary breakthrough","reasoning":"Education, pensions, child welfare, immigration control, Boer War taxation, and Haldane military reforms modestly expanded an already very strong administrative and coercive state, though Lords obstruction and Irish pressures limited elected-state capacity. Civil society also strengthened through Labour’s emergence, union legal protection after 1906, women’s suffrage mobilization, and Irish tenant/nationalist organization, but remaining exclusions and elite vetoes keep it below the ceiling.","society_change":0.2,"society_power":9.6,"state_change":0.2,"state_power":9.5}
